build_system: pnpm with Next.js build, npm-run-all2 for scripts
test_framework: Vitest with jsdom, @testing-library/react, better-sqlite3 for test DB
verification_instructions: Run pnpm type-check to verify TypeScript compiles, pnpm lint to check ESLint, pnpm test for unit tests
snapshot_or_generated_files: app/lib/generated-models/ contains generated model files - these still need JSDoc but are part of the codebase
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET - full network access. Database tests require better-sqlite3 (in-memory). No running Postgres needed for type-check/lint.
contribution_requirements: Follow existing code style with Prettier formatting. CI runs: lint (strict), type-check, test:coverage, build.
key_patterns: Files use 'use server' directive for server actions. React components are function components. Kysely for DB queries. Zod for validation schemas. Some files already have JSDoc (e.g. app/brew/schemas.ts). The project uses pnpm not npm.
relevant_files: All .ts and .tsx files in app/, root-level .ts files, migrations/, seeds/, test/
app/lib/logger.ts (class with multiple methods needing JSDoc)
directory_structure: app/ - Next.js app directory (api/, brew/, common/, lib/, manage/, navigation/, stats/, providers/); migrations/ - DB migrations; seeds/ - DB seeds; test/ - test setup and integration tests; root .ts files - config/instrumentation/middleware
